sections.paragraph: (“the justice of the people,” for עֲדֹל עָם; according to Jo. Simonis, compounded of עַד עֻלָּם; comp. عِدْوُ a hiding place; and עָלַם to hide), [Adullam], pr.n. of a city in the plain country of Judah; formerly a royal city of the Canaanites, fortified by Rehoboam, Jos 12:15 15:35 Mic 1:15 Neh 11:30. LXX. Ὀδολλάμ. Near it was מְעָרַת עֲדֻלָּם the cave of Adullam, 1Sa 22:1 2Sa 23:13 Gent. noun, עֲדֻלָּמִי Gen 38:1, 12 Gen 38:12.
